name: Railway API
what_it_does: Railway GraphQL API — deploy apps, databases, and services from GitHub or Docker with zero configuration; manage environments, variables, and deployments programmatically.
use_cases: Agents triggering deployments of new code versions via Railway API after CI passes
Provisioning ephemeral staging environments per pull request from agent workflows
Reading deployment logs and status from Railway to detect failures in agent pipelines
Managing environment variables programmatically for different deploy environments
Spinning up database services (PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB) on demand for agent workloads
not_for: Large-scale production workloads needing fine-grained infrastructure control — use AWS/GCP/Azure
Teams needing Kubernetes-native deployments — use EKS, GKE, or AKS instead
Cost-optimized high-compute workloads — Railway's pricing is per-vCPU/RAM, can be expensive at scale
best_when: Agents need to quickly deploy and manage containerized services without infrastructure expertise — Railway handles networking, TLS, and database provisioning automatically.
avoid_when: You need detailed infrastructure control, compliance certifications, or cost-optimized compute at scale.

auth.notes: API token from Railway account settings. Passed as Authorization: Bearer header. Team tokens available for organization-wide access. No scope granularity — full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $5/month in free credits; Hobby plan at $5/month includes $5 usage credits
pricing.notes: Credit card required even for free tier after trial. Idle services still incur charges. Sleepy mode available to reduce costs.

performance.rate_limits: GraphQL API: no published rate limits; webhook delivery within seconds of events

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors return standard error objects with message and extensions. Deployment failures require querying deployment logs separately. GraphQL introspection available for schema discovery.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deployment triggers create new deployments — not idempotent. Variable updates are idempotent. Service creation with same name in same project returns existing service.
known_agent_gotchas: API is GraphQL only — agents must construct GraphQL queries, not simple REST calls
Deployments are async — trigger returns deploymentId; agents must poll deployment status
Environment variables require a service restart to take effect — not applied to running containers immediately
Credit card required even for hobby accounts — blocks automated account provisioning
Railway regions are limited (US West primary) — latency implications for EU-based agent workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: API token has full account access — no scope granularity. TLS enforced. Secrets stored as environment variables (encrypted at rest). No SOC2 or compliance certifications published.
